generated_cells.notes: Markdown: title, Pokemon-vs-Digimon scene, and a 6-section roadmap mapped to the learning objectives.

Code: imports (numpy, matplotlib, scipy.ndimage), %matplotlib inline, fixed SEED/rng, E_MAX=10000, H_GRID={1..10000}; ipywidgets wrapped in install fallback with a no-op interact + show_interactive helper so all later interactive cells degrade to static. Outputs np, plt, rng, E_MAX, H_GRID, interact.
Markdown: Section 1 edge-count intuition with the p.9 e=3558 vs 7389 contrast.

Code: synthetic simple/busy grayscale images, Sobel-magnitude edge_count, 2x2 figure with e annotated; threshold FloatSlider + static fallback. Verified e(simple)=1280 < e(busy)=3420. Outputs edge_count, example_simple_e, example_busy_e.
Markdown: bridge to a simulated population (offline, ~819 vs ~971, overlapping).

Code: clipped-Gaussian D_all (Pokemon mu=4200, Digimon mu=6900, sigma=2800), shuffled, overlaid histograms. Calibration verified: h_all=4860, L_all=0.288, N_all=1790. Outputs D_all, e_all, y_all, N_all.
Markdown: Section 2, f_h definition and H with |H|=10000 as model complexity.
Code: f_h/predict, IntSlider over H_GRID redrawing the histogram with a decision-boundary line and accuracy; static fallback. Outputs f_h, predict.
Markdown: Section 3, empirical 0-1 loss with cross-entropy noted as alternative.
Code: vectorized loss_L (scalar/array h), full sweep loss_curve_all, ideal h_all/L_all marked; optional slider readout. Outputs loss_L, loss_curve_all, h_all, L_all.
Markdown: Section 4, h_train vs L(h_train,D_all) and the 0.20-vs-0.37 mislead.

Code: i.i.d. D_train, fit h_train, compare train/all/ideal losses, overlay both curves with both minima; N + resample-seed sliders, static default sets outputs. Verified gap demo runs (gap=+0.018 at N=300). Outputs sample_D_train, h_train, gap_demo.
Markdown: Section 5, bad-set definition, single-hypothesis Hoeffding + union bound -> aggregate bound.

Code: vectorized Monte-Carlo over trials, worst-case deviation on a coarse H subset (noted), empirical P(bad) vs |H|*2*exp(-2N*eps^2) across an N sweep; static render sets outputs, eps/trials sliders layered on. Verified bound is a valid upper envelope. Outputs monte_carlo_bad_prob, bound_compare_fig.
Markdown: Section 6, larger |H| lowers achievable loss but widens the gap; sum-minimizing sweet spot.

Code: |H| varied by coarsening the threshold grid; achievable loss (falling) vs Hoeffding gap term sqrt(log(2|H|/delta)/(2N)) (rising) and their sum, sweet spot marked; N/delta sliders, static fallback. Verified 12-row table, achievable 0.423->0.288. Outputs complexity_tradeoff_fig, tradeoff_table.
Markdown: recap of the arc, 3-step ML framework, Yes-Deep-Learning takeaway, and asides (single-hypothesis Hoeffding, sample complexity).

dependency_notes: No 03_cell_analysis.json existed; per-code-cell implementation details (function signatures, vectorization, error handling, widget fallbacks) were derived directly from the stage-2 goals, inputs/outputs, and widget_plan.
C02 establishes the shared imports, RNG, E_MAX/H_GRID, and show_interactive helper reused by every later code cell; strict top-to-bottom order is required.
C06 produces the fixed D_all (e_all, y_all, N_all) that is the single source of truth for C08, C10, C12, C14, and C16.
C10 defines loss_L and the ideal h_all/L_all reused by C12 (gap), C14 (Monte-Carlo), and C16 (tradeoff).

Each ipywidgets cell renders once with defaults (via show_interactive or an explicit default call) so deterministic verification needs no manual widget interaction; if ipywidgets is unavailable the identical static render is used.
Verified end-to-end on a real interpreter (no-widget fallback path): all 17 cells match stage-2 in count/order/id/type, every declared output variable is defined, and the run completes without error.
assumptions: Population D_all is simulated from the slide histograms because no image dataset is bundled; clipped-Gaussian params (mu=4200/6900, sigma=2800) were calibrated and verified to give h_all~4860 and L(h_all,D_all)~0.288, matching the slide reference (~4824 / ~0.28).

Edge detection uses numpy + scipy.ndimage (Sobel gradient magnitude, max-relative threshold) instead of OpenCV/skimage; synthetic images reproduce the simple<busy contrast (e(simple)=1280 < e(busy)=3420), not specific slide images.

C14 computes the worst-case deviation over a coarse subset of H_GRID (every 100th threshold) for responsiveness, which under-counts the true max over all 10000 hypotheses, so empirical P(bad) is a conservative estimate that still sits under the bound (noted in-cell).

C16 varies model complexity by coarsening/refining the candidate-threshold grid and uses the sample-complexity gap term eps=sqrt(log(2|H|/delta)/(2N)), exposing eps through the delta and N sliders; a faithful 1-D illustration of the |H| tradeoff rather than a multi-feature model family.
ipywidgets is assumed available on Colab; each interactive cell still falls back to a static rendering so top-to-bottom verification succeeds without manual interaction.
